shlokas.original:
सुरकार्यमिदं रम्भे कर्तव्यं सुमहत्त्वया।
लोभनं कौशिकस्येह काममोहसमन्वितम्।।1.64.1।।
shlokas.translation: "O Rambha you must entice Viswamitra by lust and passion. This important work  must be done by you in the interest of the gods".

shlokas.original:
तथोक्ता साऽप्सरा राम सहस्राक्षेण धीमता।
व्रीडिता प्राञ्जलिर्वाक्यं प्रत्युवाच सुरेश्वरम्।।1.64.2।।
shlokas.translation: O Rama, to these words of the sagacious, thousandeyed Lord of the gods (Indra) that apsara with folded hands bashfully replied:

shlokas.original:
अयं सुरपते घोरो विश्वामित्रो महामुनि:।
घोरमुत्सृजते क्रोधं मयि देव न संशय:।।1.64.3।।
ततो हि मे भयं देव प्रासादं कर्तुमर्हसि।
shlokas.translation: 'Lord of the devatas this mighty ascetic Viswamitra is a terrible person. He will ceratinly release on his dreadful anger (curse me). O Lord this is my apprehension. You should excuse me'.

shlokas.original:
एवमुक्तस्तया राम रम्भया भीतया तदा।।1.64.4।।
तामुवाच सहस्राक्षो वेपमानां कृताञ्जलिम्।
shlokas.translation: O Rama having heard Rambha trembling in fear and standing with folded palms, Indra spoke:

shlokas.original:
माभैषी रम्भे भद्रं ते कुरुष्व मम शासनम्।।1.64.5।।
कोकिलो हृदयग्राही माधवे रुचिरद्रुमे।
अहं कन्दर्पसहित स्स्थास्यामि तव पार्श्वत:।।1.64.6।।
shlokas.translation: 'O Rambha have no fear. Feel safe. Carry out my order. When the trees look so beautiful in spring I shall in captivating the form of a cuckoo, stay by your side in the company of Kamadeva (Cupid).

shlokas.original:
त्वं हि रूपं बहुगुणं कृत्वा परमभास्वरम्।
तमृषिं कौशिकं रम्भे भेदयस्व तपस्विनम्।।1.64.7।।
shlokas.translation: O Rambha displaying many (romantic) gestures and assuming a fascinating form, distract the mighty ascetic, Kausika (Viswamitra)'.

shlokas.original:
सा श्रुत्वा वचनं तस्य कृत्वा रूपमनुत्तमम्।
लोभयामास ललिता विश्वामित्रं शुचिस्मिता।।1.64.8।।
shlokas.translation: At these words (of Indra), Rambha, assumed any form of excellent beauty and with a bright smile set out to allure Viswamitra.

shlokas.original:
कोकिलस्य च शुश्राव वल्गु व्याहरत: स्वनम्।
सम्प्रहृष्टेन मनसा तत एनामुदैक्षत।।1.64.9।।
shlokas.translation: Excited by the charming warble of the cuckoo, Viswamitra opened his eyes.

shlokas.original:
अथ तस्य च शब्देन गीतेनाप्रतिमेन च।
दर्शनेन च रम्भाया मुनिस्सन्देहमागत:।।1.64.10।।
shlokas.translation: On hearing the sound of the incomparable song of the cuckoo along with the  sight of Rambha, a doubt arose in the mind of the ascetic.

shlokas.original:
सहस्राक्षस्य तत्कर्म विज्ञाय मुनिपुङ्गव:।
रम्भां क्रोधसमाविष्ट श्शशाप कुशिकात्मज:।।1.64.11।।
shlokas.translation: Son of Kushika, the eminent ascetic knew it was the work of Indra. Seized with anger,  he cursed Rambha:

shlokas.original:
यन्मां लोभयसे रम्भे कामक्रोधजयैषिणम्।
दशवर्षसहस्राणि शैली स्थास्यसि दुर्भगे ।।1.64.12।।
shlokas.translation: 'O luckless Rambha, since you have endeavoured to distract me from my desire to conquer  wrath and passion, you shall turn into a rock and remain so for ten thousand years'.

shlokas.original:
ब्राह्मण स्सुमहातेजा स्तपोबलसमन्वित:।
उद्धरिष्यति रम्भे त्वां मत्क्रोधकलुषीकृताम्।।1.64.13।।
shlokas.translation: 'O Rambha an effulgent brahmin, endowed with the power of asceticism, will liberate you from this state into which you have fallen on account of my anger'.

shlokas.original:
एवमुक्त्वा महातेजा विश्वामित्रो महामुनि:।
अशक्नुवन् धारयितुं क्रोधं सन्तापमागत:।।1.64.14।।
shlokas.translation: The brilliant Viswamitra, the great ascetic, now experienced remorse for his inability to contain anger.

shlokas.original:
तस्य शापेन महता रम्भा शैली तदाऽभवत्।
वचश्शृत्वा च कन्दर्पो महर्षेस्स च निर्गत:।।1.64.15।।
shlokas.translation: This mighty curse, of the great saint transformed Rambha into a rock and sent Kamadeva and Indra to their heels.

shlokas.original:
कोपेन स महातेजास्तपोऽपहरणे कृते।
इन्द्रियैरजितै राम न लेभे शान्तिमात्मन:।।1.64.16।।
shlokas.translation: "O Rama highly powerful Viswamitra was deprived of his ascetic merit due to his  anger. He had no peace of mind for his inability to control his senses.

shlokas.original:
बभूवास्य मनश्चिन्ता तपोऽपहरणे कृते ।
नैव क्रोधं गमिष्यामि न च वक्ष्ये कथञ्चन।।1.64.17।।
shlokas.translation: Having been deprived of his ascetic merit, he resolved not to lose his temper nor ever breathe a word".

shlokas.original:
अथवा नोच्छवसिष्यामि संवत्सरशतान्यपि।
अहं विशोषयिष्यामि ह्यात्मानं विजितेन्द्रिय:।।1.64.18।।
shlokas.translation: "Further, I shall not even inhale for hundreds of years. I shall conquer my senses and dry up this body.

shlokas.original:
तावद्यावद्धि मे प्राप्तं ब्राह्मण्यं तपसाऽऽर्जितम्।
अनुच्छवसन्नभुञ्जान स्तिष्ठेयं शाश्वतीस्समा:।।1.64.19।।
न हि मे तप्यमानस्य क्षयं यास्यन्ति मूर्तय:।
shlokas.translation: Until brahminhood is obtained by my austerities, I shall stay without breathing or eating for innumerable years. While performing penance, my limbs shall not undergo deterioration".

shlokas.original:
एवं वर्षसहस्रस्य दीक्षां स मुनिपुङ्गव:।।1.64.20।।
चकाराप्रतिमां लोके प्रतिज्ञां रघुनन्दन।
shlokas.translation: "O Descendant of Raghu in this way that eminent ascetic took an unprecedented vow to practise penance for a thousand years".    इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये बालकाण्डे चतुष्षष्टितमस्सर्ग:।।  Thus ends the sixtyfourth sarga of Balakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.
